_comment: Single source of truth for desktop-build.ps1 and desktop-quit.ps1. Copy this file to scripts\app-it.config.json and customize. Mirrors the macOS app-it.config.example.json schema exactly — the same fields work on both platforms. The optional platform.windows block carries Windows-only specifics without polluting the shared schema.

_fields.name: Display name. Non-ASCII OK (e.g. 'Momó Studio'). The build script uses this as the .exe name, the desktop\ folder name, and the Start Menu shortcut label.
_fields.slug: File-safe slug. Lowercase, hyphens. Used for assets\<slug>-icon.{png,svg} and to namespace runtime/log paths under %LOCALAPPDATA%\app-it\<slug>\.
_fields.port: Preferred port. The launcher tries this first; if busy, scans [port..port+50] for a free port. Records actual runtime port to %LOCALAPPDATA%\app-it\<slug>\server.port.
_fields.start_command: Command to start the dev server, run from PROJECT_ROOT. Must honor PORT env var. Vite needs --port via CLI: 'npm run dev -- --port $PORT'. Avoid hardcoded -p literals.
_fields.bundle_id: Reverse-DNS bundle identifier. Retained as a stable per-app key even though Windows has no LaunchServices — it namespaces state/log paths under %LOCALAPPDATA%\app-it\<slug>\ the same way it does on macOS.
_fields.version: Marketing version. Doesn't matter for unsigned use; pick anything semver-shaped.
_fields.polyfill_path: Optional absolute path to a JS polyfill file (FSA shim). Use @ROOT@ to reference the repo root: '@ROOT@\assets\<slug>-polyfill.js'. Empty string when no polyfill is needed.
_fields.backend_port: Optional. Set for multi-server cohabiting apps. The backend service's preferred port. The launcher allocates a free port starting here for the backend, exports it as API_PORT to both processes.
_fields.backend_start_command: Optional. The command to start the backend service. Must honor API_PORT env var.

platform.windows._comment: Optional block. Every field has a sensible default — a macOS config opened on Windows works without it.
platform.windows.webview2_user_data_dir: Where WebView2 stores its profile (cookies, cache, local storage). Supports %LOCALAPPDATA% and %APPDATA% expansions. Default: %LOCALAPPDATA%\app-it\<slug>\WebView2
platform.windows.ico_sizes: Pixel sizes to include in the multi-resolution .ico. Default [16,32,48,64,128,256]. Rationale for defaults: 16/32/48 cover legacy taskbar and DPI contexts; 64/128 add mid-DPI taskbar and Start Menu rendering for nearly free; 256 covers high-DPI and Explorer thumbnails.
platform.windows.start_menu_folder: Name of the folder under Start Menu\Programs that holds the .lnk shortcut. Default: 'app-it'. Mirrors macOS '~/Applications/App It/' as the grouping point for all app-it launchers.
platform.windows.edge_fallback: Set to true to force the Edge --app= launcher for this app even when the .NET SDK and WebView2 are available. Default: false. The build script auto-selects this when dotnet publish fails.
